title: Exponential  vs. linear models
description: Given a description of a real-world relationship, determine whether that relationship is linear or exponential.

assessment_items.question_content:
The table represents the cost of buying a small piece of land in a remote village since the year $1990$.

**Which kind of function best models this relationship?**

Time (years)| Cost (thousands of dollars)
- | - | -
$0$ | $30$ 
$2$ | $36.9$ 
$4$ | $44.1$
$6$ | $51.1$
$8$ | $57.9$
$10$ | $65.1$

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Linear and exponential relationships differ in the way the $y$-values change when the $x$-values increase by a constant amount:

- In a linear relationship, the $y$-values have *constant differences*.
- In an exponential relationship, the $y$-values have *constant ratios*.

The time increases by $2$ years in each row of the table.

The costs increase with an approximately constant difference.

$\begin{align}
36.9-30 &\approx 44.1-36.9 \\\\
&\approx 65.1-57.9 \\\\
& \approx 7
\end{align}$
A linear function best models this relationship.

choices.content: Linear
Exponential

assessment_items.question_content:
The table represents the number of solar panels sold in a sunny city since January of $2010$.

**Which kind of function best models this relationship?**

Time (months)| Solar panels
- | - | -
$0$ | $320$ 
$4$ | $640$ 
$8$ | $950$
$12$ | $1280$
$16$ | $1610$
$20$ | $1920$

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Linear and exponential relationships differ in the way the $y$-values change when the $x$-values increase by a constant amount:

- In a linear relationship, the $y$-values have *constant differences*.
- In an exponential relationship, the $y$-values have *constant ratios*.

The time increases by $4$ months in each row of the table.

The number of solar panels increases with an approximately constant difference.

$\begin{align}
640-320 &\approx 1280-950 \\\\
&\approx 1920-1610 \\\\
& \approx 320
\end{align}$
A linear function best models this relationship.

choices.content: Linear
Exponential

assessment_items.question_content:
The table represents the number of burgers sold by a restaurant since it was opened.

Time (months)| Burgers sold
- | - | -
$1$ | $353$ 
$4$ | $691$ 
$7$ | $1034$
$10$ | $1374$
$13$ | $1713$

**Which graph best represents the relationship between time and the number of burgers sold?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Linear and exponential relationships differ in the way the $y$-values change when the $x$-values increase by a constant amount:

- In a linear relationship, the $y$-values have *constant differences*.
- In an exponential relationship, the $y$-values have *constant ratios*.

The time increases by $3$ months in each row of the table.

The number of burgers sold increases with an approximately constant difference.

$\begin{align}
691-353&\approx 1374-1034 \\\\
&\approx 1713-1374 \\\\
& \approx 340
\end{align}$

Every $3$ months, the number of burgers sold grows by an approximately constant **amount**, so it is best to use a linear graph to model it.

A linear graph appears as a straight line.

The graph that best represents the relationship between time and the number of burgers sold is:

![An increasing graph with a constant, positive slope.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4aafab3449c2237261075c22c8067310d6c7731a)

choices.content: ![An increasing graph with a constant, positive slope.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4aafab3449c2237261075c22c8067310d6c7731a)
![An increasing graph that curves more steeply as x increases.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/51e01ed550d02d785b066d3b55a8d3b8fcd5df3b)

assessment_items.question_content:
The table represents the number of milkshakes sold by a restaurant since they were added to the menu.

Time (months)| Milkshakes sold
- | - | -
$1$ | $259$ 
$4$ | $380$ 
$7$ | $501$
$10$ | $622$
$13$ | $744$

**Which graph best represents the relationship between time and the number of milkshakes sold?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Linear and exponential relationships differ in the way the $y$-values change when the $x$-values increase by a constant amount:

- In a linear relationship, the $y$-values have *constant differences*.
- In an exponential relationship, the $y$-values have *constant ratios*.

The time increases by $3$ months in each row of the table.

The number of milkshakes sold increases with an approximately constant difference.

$\begin{align}
380-259 &\approx 622-501 \\\\
&\approx 744 - 622 \\\\
&\approx 121
\end{align}$

Every $3$ months, the number of milkshakes sold grows by an approximately constant **amount**, so it is best to use a linear graph to model it.

A linear graph appears as a straight line.

The graph that best represents the relationship between time and the number of milkshakes sold is:

![An increasing graph with a constant, positive slope.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4aafab3449c2237261075c22c8067310d6c7731a)

choices.content: ![An increasing graph with a constant, positive slope.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4aafab3449c2237261075c22c8067310d6c7731a)
![An increasing graph that curves more steeply as x increases.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/51e01ed550d02d785b066d3b55a8d3b8fcd5df3b)

assessment_items.question_content:
The table gives the number of branches on a large tree after the year $2000$.

**Which kind of function best models this relationship?**

Time (years)| Branches
- | - | -
$0$ | $16$ 
$2$ | $23$ 
$4$ | $33$
$6$ | $48$
$8$ | $69$
$10$ | $99$

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Linear and exponential relationships differ in the way the $y$-values change when the $x$-values increase by a constant amount:

- In a linear relationship, the $y$-values have *constant differences*.
- In an exponential relationship, the $y$-values have *constant ratios*.

The time increases by $2$ years in each row of the table.

The numbers of branches increase by an approximately constant ratio.

$\begin{align}
\dfrac{23}{16} &\approx \dfrac{33}{23} \\\\
&\approx \dfrac{99}{69} \\\\
& \approx 1.4
\end{align}$
An exponential function best models this relationship.

choices.content: Linear
Exponential

assessment_items.question_content:
The table gives the number of views on an interesting video after it has been uploaded.

**Which kind of function best models this relationship?**

Time (months)| Views  
- | - | -
$0$ | $50$ 
$2$ | $313$ 
$4$ | $1950$
$6$ | $12{,}210$
$8$ | $76{,}300$
$10$ | $476{,}800$

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Linear and exponential relationships differ in the way the $y$-values change when the $x$-values increase by a constant amount:

- In a linear relationship, the $y$-values have *constant differences*.
- In an exponential relationship, the $y$-values have *constant ratios*.

The time increases by $2$ months in each row of the table.

The number of views increases by an approximately constant ratio.

$\begin{align}
\dfrac{313}{50} &\approx \dfrac{1950}{313} \\\\
&\approx \dfrac{476{,}800}{76{,}300} \\\\
& \approx 6.25
\end{align}$
An exponential function best models this relationship.

choices.content: Linear
Exponential

assessment_items.question_content:
The table gives predictions of the average costs of a gasoline car after the year $2030$.

|| **Gasoline car** ||
Time (years)| Cost (thousands of dollars)
- | - | -
$0$ | $35$ 
$5$ | $40.2$ 
$10$ | $46.3$
$15$ | $53.2$
$20$ | $61.2$

**Which graph best represents the relationship between time and the predicted cost?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Linear and exponential relationships differ in the way the $y$-values change when the $x$-values increase by a constant amount:

- In a linear relationship, the $y$-values have *constant differences*.
- In an exponential relationship, the $y$-values have *constant ratios*.

The time increases by $5$ years in each row of the table.

The predicted cost increases by an approximately constant ratio.

$\begin{align}
\dfrac{40.2}{35} &\approx \dfrac{46.3}{40.2} \\\\
&\approx \dfrac{61.2}{53.2} \\\\
& \approx 1.15
\end{align}$

Every $5$ years, the predicted cost grows by an approximately constant **ratio**, so it is best to use an exponential graph to model it.

An increasing exponential graph curves more steeply upward as $x$ increases.

The graph that best represents the relationship between time and the predicted cost is:

![An increasing graph that curves more steeply as x increases.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/51e01ed550d02d785b066d3b55a8d3b8fcd5df3b)

choices.content: ![An increasing graph with a constant, positive slope.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4aafab3449c2237261075c22c8067310d6c7731a)
![An increasing graph that curves more steeply as x increases.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/51e01ed550d02d785b066d3b55a8d3b8fcd5df3b)

assessment_items.question_content:
The table gives the average mass of a sunfish after it is born.

|| **Sunfish** ||
Time (weeks)| Average mass (milligrams)
- | - | -
$0$ | $3.5$ 
$5$ | $19.3$ 
$10$ | $106.5$
$15$ | $587.5$
$20$ | $3240.5$

**Which graph best represents the relationship between time and the average mass?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Linear and exponential relationships differ in the way the $y$-values change when the $x$-values increase by a constant amount:

- In a linear relationship, the $y$-values have *constant differences*.
- In an exponential relationship, the $y$-values have *constant ratios*.

The time increases by $5$ weeks in each row of the table.

The average mass increases by an approximately constant ratio.

$\begin{align}
\dfrac{19.3}{3.5} &\approx \dfrac{106.5}{19.3} \\\\
&\approx \dfrac{3240.5}{587.5} \\\\
& \approx 5.5
\end{align}$

Every $5$ weeks, the average mass grows by an approximately constant **ratio**, so it is best to use an exponential graph to model it.

An increasing exponential graph curves more steeply upward as $x$ increases.

The graph that best represents the relationship between time and the predicted mass is:

![An increasing graph that curves more steeply as x increases.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/51e01ed550d02d785b066d3b55a8d3b8fcd5df3b)

choices.content: ![An increasing graph with a constant, positive slope.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4aafab3449c2237261075c22c8067310d6c7731a)
![An increasing graph that curves more steeply as x increases.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/51e01ed550d02d785b066d3b55a8d3b8fcd5df3b)
